text:
BDSM role terminology and mental-state terms
The article distinguishes a top or dominant as the partner who performs or controls an activity and a bottom or submissive as the partner who receives or is controlled. It calls people who change between top or dominant and bottom or submissive roles switches, whether the change happens within one relationship or between relationships.
The article says many BDSM activities involve an exchange of power and control without pain or humiliation. It describes a corresponding trance-like state as subspace for a submissive and domspace for a dominant, in a passage that also mentions endorphin effects comparable to a runner's high or post-orgasm afterglow.
title: BDSM - Wikipedia
